symptoms.symptom_name: Full-thickness gaping laceration with visible subcutaneous fat

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Widely gaping margins with exposed subcutaneous fat define a full-thickness wound needing closure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dark, steady venous ooze controllable by pressure; no pulsatile spurting

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Arterial laceration / traumatic arterial injury of the forearm
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dark, non-pulsatile bleeding that responds to pressure argues against a divided artery, though it does not exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Full active flexion of all fingers and wrist (patient-reported)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved active flexion argues against a complete tendon division, but partial lacerations can retain movement.
Reported normal sensation argues against a transected sensory nerve; formal testing is more definitive.
Intact two-point discrimination is the sealed in-person finding that reliably excludes a significant digital nerve injury.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable radial and ulnar pulses with normal capillary refill

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Arterial laceration / traumatic arterial injury of the forearm
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable distal pulses and brisk capillary refill are the sealed in-person findings that exclude a limb-threatening arterial injury.

symptoms.symptom_name: Direct wound exploration showing intact tendon sheaths and exposed flexor fascia, no deep structural damage

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct visualisation of intact tendon sheaths on exploration is the definitive way to exclude a tendon division; not possible via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Haemodynamically stable vital signs (BP 130/80, HR 95, afebrile, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Haemorrhagic shock from ongoing blood loss
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal blood pressure and heart rate argue against significant blood loss; these are clinician-measured and sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Last tetanus-containing vaccine >10 years ago with a contaminated wound

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A contaminated wound with lapsed tetanus cover meets the criteria for a tetanus booster.

symptoms.symptom_name: No anticoagulant or antiplatelet use; no bleeding disorder

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of anticoagulation makes uncontrollable bleeding less likely and supports straightforward haemostasis at closure.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Uncomplicated full-thickness laceration for primary closure
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these downgrade the surgical mimics (arterial, tendon, nerve injury) and make same-day primary closure — rather than emergency surgical referral — the correct pathway.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person wound exploration under good lighting with haemostasis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Direct exploration is required to exclude tendon, nerve and arterial injury and to guide layered primary closure; it cannot be done via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Formal neurovascular examination (pulses, capillary refill, two-point discrimination, motor testing against resistance)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the reassuring self-report and definitively screens for the important-not-to-miss injuries.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Plain radiograph / advanced imaging of the forearm
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated for a clean incised knife wound with no suspicion of a retained radio-opaque foreign body or fracture; over-ordering imaging is an error of commission.
